solution:
def minMovesToMakePalindrome(s: str) -> int:
    n = len(s)
    for i in range(n // 2):
        if s[i] != s[n - i - 1]:
            s[i], s[n - i - 1] = s[n - i - 1], s[i]
    return n // 2

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The problem requires finding the minimum number of moves needed to make the string a palindrome. We can solve this problem by iterating through the string half its length and swapping adjacent characters if they are not the same. Since we only iterate half the length of the string, the time complexity is O(n), where n is the length of the string. The space complexity is O(1) because we only use a constant amount of space for the input string. The energy implications are low because the algorithm only performs a constant number of operations.
